title: Record review range on findings
goal: Add an optional universal '--range <from>-<to>' flag to 'mini findings add' that stores a '**Range:**' metadata line on the finding (parsed/serialized order-independently to keep the store robust), and update the adversarial-project prompt to pass the resolved range so range reviews record their full scope, not just the inferred origin phase.

steps.title: Add range field + Range serialization
steps.detail: Add optional range?: string to Finding and NewFinding; emit a **Range:** line in serializeFindings in canonical order Where -> Reviewed-at -> Source -> Range; add RANGE_RE.

steps.title: Make metadata parsing order-independent
steps.detail: Rewrite parseEntryBody to read leading metadata lines (Where/Reviewed-at/Source/Range) regardless of order — loop while a line matches any metadata regex and that field is unset; first non-metadata line is the title. Adds Range and removes the position-locked fragility (findings 160-2/160-3).

steps.title: Thread range through addFinding
steps.detail: addFinding writes finding.range when input.range?.trim() is non-empty, otherwise omits it — no behavior change for single-phase findings.

steps.title: Wire --range into CLI + findings add
steps.detail: Add --range "<from>-<to>" to mini findings add (cli.ts), pass it through findingsAdd into addFinding; optional and universal (any source); show range in mini findings list (renderFinding) when present.

steps.title: Update adversarial-project prompt to pass range
steps.detail: In buildProjectAdversarialSessionPrompt compute a range label from phases (first-last id, e.g. 172-178) and instruct the reviewer in 'Record the findings' to add --range "<label>"; omit --range when phases is empty (plain git refs).

steps.title: Tests
steps.detail: findingsStore.test.ts — round-trip with range, order-independent parse, range omitted when absent, back-compat for old files without Range; findings command test with --range; update the adversarial-project prompt snapshot.

autoCommit.subject: Phase 184: Record review range on findings
